1._: Barab pauses at the threshold to be seen before he enters, as is right among his people — a lean, weathered stranger in road-worn hides, his skin and his manner not of this country. He marks the carved counting-marks on the mantel, the maps weighted on the table, the keeper's measuring look. And on the draught through the door he catches the smell of the high cold he knows too well — the cold he had hoped to leave behind the mountains.

content._: barab's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
